howItWorks.intro: This tariff savings calculator uses established formulas to provide accurate results.
rules: Current Total = Annual Spend × (1 + Current Tariff%)
Alt Total = (Annual Spend × (1 + Price Premium%)) × (1 + Alt Tariff%)
howItWorks.outro: Results are estimates based on standard formulas. Verify with current local data for your specific situation.

faq.question: How can I reduce tariff costs?
faq.answer: Options include: sourcing from free trade agreement (FTA) countries, using foreign trade zones, tariff engineering (reclassifying products), first-sale valuation, and applying for tariff exclusions.

faq.question: What are the major US free trade agreements?
faq.answer: USMCA (Canada/Mexico), KORUS (South Korea), CAFTA-DR (Central America/DR), Australia FTA, Singapore FTA, Chile FTA, and others. Products qualifying under these may enter duty-free.

faq.question: Is it worth switching suppliers to save on tariffs?
faq.answer: If savings exceed 5-10% of annual spend, it's usually worth investigating. Factor in quality differences, lead times, minimum orders, intellectual property risks, and supplier transition costs.

faq.question: Is the Tariff Savings Calculator free to use?
faq.answer: Yes, completely free with no signup required. Use it as many times as you need — there are no limits or hidden fees.

faq.question: How accurate is this calculator?
faq.answer: This calculator uses standard practical calculation formulas trusted by users. Results are reliable estimates for planning purposes. For critical decisions, we recommend consulting a qualified professional to verify.

sections.title: What Is the Tariff Savings Calculator?
sections.body: The Tariff Savings Calculator is a free online tool designed for users who need quick, accurate calculations in the practical calculation space. By entering your annual import spend, current tariff rate, alternative country tariff, you get instant results including current annual total, alternative annual total, annual savings. No formulas to memorize, no spreadsheets to build — just enter your numbers and get the answer in seconds. Whether you're a beginner or experienced professional, this calculator saves you time and eliminates guesswork.

sections.title: Why This Calculation Matters
sections.body: Getting current annual total right can make the difference between success and costly mistakes. In practical calculation, small errors compound quickly. Manual calculations are error-prone and time-consuming, especially under pressure. This calculator applies proven formulas used by users worldwide, giving you confidence that your numbers are correct. Use it to get accurate results with precision and avoid common pitfalls that trip up beginners.

sections.title: When Should You Use This Calculator?
sections.body: This tool is most useful when you know your annual import spend and need to find the right current annual total. It's also great for quick estimates before committing to a decision, and to double-check manual calculations or professional quotes, and when comparing different scenarios side by side. Bookmark this page and come back whenever you need a fast, reliable answer — the calculator is always free and requires no signup.

howToSteps.title: Enter Your Annual Import Spend ($)
howToSteps.description: Start by entering your annual import spend — this is the primary input for the calculation.

howToSteps.title: Fill In Additional Details
howToSteps.description: Complete the remaining fields: current tariff rate, alternative country tariff, alternative price premium, one-time transition cost. Each value refines the calculation for greater accuracy.

howToSteps.title: Click Calculate
howToSteps.description: Hit the Calculate button to run the numbers. Results appear instantly below.

howToSteps.title: Review Your Results
howToSteps.description: Check your current annual total, alternative annual total, annual savings. Use these figures to inform your next decision or compare against alternative scenarios.

tips: Double-check your annual import spend before calculating — even small input errors can significantly change your results.
Run the calculator with different values to compare scenarios and find the optimal approach for your situation.
Pay attention to both current annual total and alternative annual total — they work together to give you the full picture.
Bookmark this page for quick access next time you need to get accurate results.
If you're unsure about your one-time transition cost, start with a conservative estimate and adjust from there.
